beats.activity: Wake up, water plants (including Houdini), morning coffee and toast.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus wakes early as is his habit, tends to his plants with care—a grounding ritual—before facing a grey, rainy Tuesday. Low energy from yesterday (45/100) means he'll keep this morning routine calm and contained at home.

beats.activity: Drive to SoDo branch in light rain, taking surface streets to avoid SR-520 closure eastbound.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library - SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; Cyrus departs early to avoid the snarl and arrives before the closure window. Rain from 7am–2pm means he's already wet, so he drives rather than taking transit. Surface streets add ~50 min buffer to his usual commute.

beats.activity: Cataloging, reference desk, helping patrons with research queries. Indoor work keeps him dry.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library - SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: A long morning stretch at the library while rain pounds outside. Cyrus is content with quiet reference work; yesterday's match-day buzz has faded into his normal skeptical, steady rhythm. The closure doesn't affect him at the library.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby café, staying dry indoors. Soup and sandwich.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady at noon. Rather than venture far, Cyrus grabs a quick warm meal at a familiar spot he can walk to; Pike Place is a short walk from SoDo. Warm food suits the grey, cool weather and his modest energy level.

beats.activity: Walk back to library through light drizzle; rain clearing by early afternoon.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library - SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Rain is tapering off by 2pm. Cyrus takes a slow walk back, letting the weather settle. A moment to himself between lunch and the afternoon shift.

beats.activity: Reference desk and cataloging work through the afternoon. Patrons filtering in as weather clears.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library - SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: The afternoon shift is steady and quiet—Cyrus is a skeptical librarian who finds satisfaction in the work itself, not in big social moments. Low energy means he stays put, focusing on tasks.

beats.activity: Drive home from SoDo, surface streets clear of morning traffic now.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute is straightforward; the SR-520 closure is long over. Cyrus drives home as the sky clears, looking forward to a quiet evening.

beats.activity: Prepare a simple dinner—pasta, vegetables. Check on plants again.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus cooks for himself at home, a quiet ritual. He's modest in energy and prefers solitude tonight. The plants get a second check—they're his companions.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: knitting by the window, chess podcast, one online game against a friend.
beats.reasoning: Low energy and skeptical temperament mean Cyrus winds down at home with his hobbies. Knitting needles click, a chess podcast plays softly. He values close friendships, so an online chess game with someone he knows feels right. No crowds, no noise.
Cyrus winds down early after a quiet, rainy day. Seven and a half hours of sleep restores him for Wednesday. The day ends where it began—at home, content.

diary: Woke to that familiar Seattle grey, the kind that makes you grateful for indoor work. Watered Houdini—my temperamental orchid—before coffee. The SR-520 closure had me taking surface streets this morning, which meant dodging the usual cross-lake chaos. Spent most of the day at the SoDo branch doing what I love: helping a woman track down obscure Pacific Northwest history sources, cataloging donations. Grabbed soup at Piroshky on my way back; the rain was already softening by then. Afternoon picked up as the weather cleared—patrons trickling in like the clouds were finally parting. By evening I was home, stirring pasta and watching the light fade, needles clicking through a half-finished scarf. Played chess online with an old friend later. Quiet Tuesday. Exactly what I needed.
thought_process: The bridge closure didn't really rattle me—I've lived in Seattle long enough to have backup routes memorized. If anything, the surface streets felt less frantic than the usual morning rush. I chose to stay put at the library most of the day because the weather made it obvious; why fight the rain when I'm most useful indoors helping people anyway? By afternoon, watching the clouds thin out, I felt my mood lift naturally. The commute home was smooth, and I think that ease carried into the evening. I opted for knitting and chess instead of anything demanding—something about rainy Tuesdays makes me want to slow down, be methodical. Just needed that rhythm tonight.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
